articles.title: GBP/USD nears 1.3590 as softer US PPI sinks Dollar demand
articles.expand: The Pound Sterling (GBP) advances on Tuesday as traders remain optimistic about a possible resolution to the US-Iran conflict. At the same time, the US Dollar (USD) weakens amid a hot US inflation report that missed forecasts of a higher print.

articles.title: ECB's Makhlouf: Not yet seeing changes in consumer behaviour from higher inflation
articles.expand: It's not impossible to see the Fed and ECB take different paths in the short term We are absolutely focused on delivering on inflation target If shocks take inflation off target but not persistently, we should be measured in our response How measured of a response is the question? Does that mean not moving rates at all or less than they would other...

articles.title: USD/JPY tumbles as softer US data and BoJ hawkish tilt collide with shifting war sentiment
articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair is trading with a bearish bias near the 158.90 region on Tuesday, extending recent losses as the US Dollar (USD) continues to soften amid improving risk sentiment on renewed hopes of United States (US)-Iran negotiations.

articles.title: Asia FX: Mixed implications of Strait of Hormuz risk – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Senior Currency Analyst Michael Wan highlights that Trump has begun a US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, but risk assets have rebounded as talks between the US and Iran continue.

articles.title: ECB’s President Lagarde: We have to be data dependent
articles.expand: Christine Lagarde, President of the European Central Bank (ECB), spoke to Bloomberg TV on Tuesday. She claimed that they need to keep an eye on the medium term while checking data.

articles.title: USD/CAD pares losses as Oil slides below $90 on US-Iran talks optimism
articles.expand: USD/CAD trims some of its earlier losses on Tuesday as easing Oil prices on renewed US-Iran talks optimism weigh on the commodity-linked Canadian Dollar (CAD), helping the pair recover from intraday lows even as improving risk sentiment keeps the US Dollar (USD) under broad pressure.

articles.title: Bessent says Fed should "wait and see" before cutting rates
articles.expand: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent crossed the wires on Tuesday, stating that he’s “quite confident” that core prices would continue to edge lower in the US despite the Iran war, and that he’s pressing the Federal Reserve (Fed) to cut rates.

articles.title: GBP/USD: Upside bias with limited resistance – Scotiabank
articles.expand: Scotiabank notes the Pound is up 0.3% versus the Dollar, trading at pre‑conflict highs as investors welcome strong demand for UK debt. Domestic data risk is light before trade and industrial production, while BoE speakers, including Gov. Bailey, pose event risk.

articles.title: Lagarde Says Too Early to Call ECB Rate Path Despite Energy Surge
articles.expand: ECB President Christine Lagarde pushed back against rising market expectations for near-term rate hikes, signaling caution despite the energy shock from the Middle East conflict. articles.title: White House Adviser Hassett: Outlook for Fed having room to cut rates is going to be very solid
articles.expand: Kevin Hassett, Senior Adviser for the White House told Fox Business on Tuesday that they expect a rapid reduction in energy prices once the Strait of Hormuz is reopened.

articles.title: Bessent: Trump hasn't opted to raise 10% tariff to 15% "at this time"
articles.expand: When US tariffs were struck down by a 6-3 decision in the Supreme Court, the President announced a 10% global tariff under a different authority immediately. articles.title: USD/CAD: Bearish setup as valuation gap narrows – Scotiabank
articles.expand: Scotiabank strategists Shaun Osborne and Eric Theoret note the Canadian Dollar (CAD) is making modest gains versus a softer US Dollar (USD), though it lags core majors and weakens on crosses. They estimate USD/CAD equilibrium at 1.3527, with undervaluation correcting mainly via Dollar softness.

articles.title: ECB's Lagarde: We are between the baseline and adverse scenarios
articles.expand: The ECB needs to take a medium-term view Need to keep eyes on the medium term while checking data daily We have to be completely agile We have to be data dependent The captain won't leave the ship with clouds on the horizon This article was written by Adam Button at investinglive.com.

articles.title: GBP/JPY extends rally to 2008 highs as elevated Oil prices weigh on the Yen
articles.expand: GBP/JPY advances on Tuesday, extending gains for a seventh consecutive day as elevated Oil prices continue to weigh on the Japanese Yen (JPY), while improving market sentiment, driven by renewed hopes of US-Iran negotiations, supports the British Pound (GBP).

articles.title: Dollar Selloff Intensifies as Soft PPI Eases Fed Pressure, US-Iran Optimism Builds
articles.expand: Dollar is being hit from both sides—soft inflation and warmer diplomacy. A cooler-than-expected PPI reading has eased pressure on the Federal Reserve to turn more hawkish, while renewed optimism around US-Iran negotiations is unwinding the war premium that briefly supported the greenback. Together, these forces are driving an extended, broad-based ...

articles.title: Gulf region: Shock resilience and investment outlook – BNP Paribas
articles.expand: BNP Paribas’ Stéphane ALBY assesses how Gulf economies are absorbing the conflict-related shock. He notes that Oil exports via the Strait of Hormuz have been severely disrupted, with Bahrain, Kuwait, and Qatar hit hardest, while Saudi Arabia and the UAE partially benefit from higher Oil prices.

articles.title: Japan's central bank cools rate hike expectations, removing a key risk for bitcoin's rally
articles.expand: The BOJ's dovish shift keeps the yen carry trade alive, the same trade whose unwind crashed bitcoin 24% in two days in August 2024.

articles.title: ASX 200 Climbs 0.6% as Energy Stocks Surge Amid US-Iran Hormuz Blockade Tensions
articles.expand: SYDNEY — The S&P/ASX 200 index rose 52.5 points, or 0.59%, to close at 8,978.5 on Tuesday, clawing back ground as soaring oil prices lifted energy shares even as broader market caution persisted over escalating U.S.-Iran tensions in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.expand: The Japanese Yen (JPY) gains against the US Dollar (USD) as easing oil prices fade stagflation concerns in Japan. However, the recent surge in energy costs linked to Middle East concerns, fueled expectations of a near-term Bank of Japan (BoJ) rate hike.
